vocabulary.example: I hope to be recognized for my work.
She expects to be invited to the party.
I want to be promoted soon.

vocabulary.translation: need → infinitivo pasivo (foco en la acción)
vocabulary.example: This machine needs to be repaired urgently.
The problem deserves to be discussed in more detail.
All applicants are required to be informed in advance.
We arranged to be met at the airport.
The system doesn't seem to be updated with the latest software.
She agreed to be interviewed by the committee.
He chose to be represented by a lawyer.
She prefers to be called by her first name.
I hate to be the last one to know.
I can't afford to go on holiday.
She managed to finish the report on time.
He refused to sign the contract.
They failed to submit the documents on time.
She promised to call as soon as she arrived.
He pretended not to notice the mistake.
I really enjoyed being involved in the project.
She avoids being seen in public.

vocabulary.translation: remember → gerundio pasivo (recuerdo de algo recibido)
vocabulary.example: I remember being told I wasn't clever enough.
I don't recall being informed about this issue.

vocabulary.translation: hate → gerundio pasivo (proceso en sí, sin gustar)
vocabulary.example: My nephew hates being teased by his classmates.
She doesn't mind being asked difficult questions.
They considered being transferred to another branch.
I dislike being interrupted when I'm speaking.
They risk being fined if they don't comply.
She kept being asked the same question.
He always insisted on being called 'Sir'.
She succeeded in being selected for the position.
He admitted making a mistake in the report.
She denied knowing anything about the incident.
Have you finished writing the proposal?
They suggested reducing traffic in the city centre.
The report recommends introducing stricter regulations.
This proposal involves improving cycling infrastructure.
They considered reducing traffic in the city centre.
She kept putting off making the decision.
He gave up trying to fix the machine.
Do you fancy going out for dinner tonight?
She can't stand being kept waiting.
I can't help feeling that something is wrong.
You should practise speaking in front of a mirror.
I can't imagine living without access to the internet.

vocabulary.translation: by + gerundio / medio para lograr algo
vocabulary.example: This can be achieved by investing in public transport.
They left without informing anyone about the decision.

vocabulary.translation: instead of + gerundio / en lugar de hacer algo
vocabulary.example: Instead of using cars, people could cycle to work.

vocabulary.translation: after + gerundio / después de hacer algo
vocabulary.example: After reviewing the data, we reached a conclusion.

vocabulary.translation: before + gerundio / antes de hacer algo
vocabulary.example: Before submitting the proposal, check all the headings.

vocabulary.translation: for + gerundio / razón o propósito (❌ nunca for + to-inf)
vocabulary.example: The city is known for investing heavily in green technology.

vocabulary.translation: in spite of + gerundio / a pesar de hacer algo
vocabulary.example: In spite of having read the instructions twice, I couldn't understand it.
On arriving at the hotel, he went straight to his room.

vocabulary.translation: about + gerundio / a punto de / referido a
vocabulary.example: She was worried about being left out of the project.

vocabulary.translation: of + gerundio / después de sustantivo o adjetivo
vocabulary.example: There is a risk of being misunderstood if the tone is too formal.

vocabulary.translation: remember + -ing → recuerdo de algo ya hecho
vocabulary.example: I remember being told I wasn't clever enough. (= recuerdo que me lo dijeron)

vocabulary.translation: remember + to-inf → acordarse de hacer algo (obligación)
vocabulary.example: Did you remember to ring Nigel? (= ¿te acordaste de llamar?)
She stopped crying as soon as she saw her mother.

vocabulary.translation: stop + to-inf → pararse para hacer otra cosa
vocabulary.example: We stopped to buy some water at the motorway service area.

vocabulary.translation: try + -ing → probar a hacer algo (a ver qué pasa)
vocabulary.example: I tried searching the web and finally found an address.

vocabulary.translation: try + to-inf → intentar hacer algo (con dificultad)
vocabulary.example: I tried to email Simon but it bounced back.

vocabulary.translation: go on + -ing → continuar haciendo lo mismo
vocabulary.example: He went on singing after everyone else had finished.

vocabulary.translation: go on + to-inf → pasar a hacer algo diferente
vocabulary.example: She recited a poem, then went on to sing a lovely folk song.
Working in London means leaving home at 6.30.

vocabulary.translation: mean + to-inf → tener la intención de
vocabulary.example: I didn't mean to make you cry.

vocabulary.translation: regret + -ing → lamentarse de algo ya hecho
vocabulary.example: I regret saying that — it was unkind.

vocabulary.translation: regret + to-inf → lamentar tener que decir algo (formal)
vocabulary.example: We regret to inform you that your application was unsuccessful.
Running to the bus stop, he tripped over.

vocabulary.translation: participio pasado / cláusula pasiva reducida → past participle
vocabulary.example: Directed by Spielberg, the movie was a huge success.

vocabulary.translation: participio perfecto / acción anterior → having + past participle
vocabulary.example: Having studied at university, I got a job in research.

vocabulary.translation: participio perfecto pasivo → having been + past participle
vocabulary.example: Having been educated at Eton, he had a good start in life.

vocabulary.translation: participio presente pasivo → being + past participle
vocabulary.example: Being trained as a doctor, she was often asked to help in emergencies.
❌ They suggested to reduce traffic. ✅ They suggested reducing traffic.

vocabulary.translation: Tras preposición SIEMPRE -ing (by, without, instead of, for...)
vocabulary.example: ❌ This can be achieved by invest more money. ✅ by investing more money.

vocabulary.word: ❌ needs to address → ✅ needs to be addressed
vocabulary.translation: need/deserve/require + to be + past participle (pasivo, no activo)
vocabulary.example: ❌ This issue needs to address. ✅ This issue needs to be addressed.

vocabulary.translation: for + -ing SIEMPRE (❌ for + to-infinitive es calco del español)
vocabulary.example: ❌ for improve safety. ✅ for improving safety.

vocabulary.word: ❌ the aim is improve → ✅ the aim is to improve
vocabulary.example: ❌ The aim is improve traffic. ✅ The aim is to improve traffic.

vocabulary.word: ❌ people is used to drive → ✅ people are used to driving
vocabulary.translation: be used to + -ing (estar acostumbrado), NOT to-infinitive
vocabulary.example: ❌ people is used to drive. ✅ people are used to driving.

vocabulary.word: ❌ this needs being solved → ✅ this needs to be solved
vocabulary.translation: need + to be + past participle (pasivo formal) / need + -ing (compacto, informal)
vocabulary.example: ❌ This needs being solved. ✅ This needs to be solved. / This needs solving.
❌ They left without to inform anyone. ✅ without informing anyone.

vocabulary.word: ❌ people should be encouraged to be helped → ✅ encouraged to help
vocabulary.translation: No doble pasivo innecesario: encouraged to + active infinitive
vocabulary.example: ❌ People should be encouraged to be helped. ✅ People should be encouraged to help.

vocabulary.word: ❌ gain a scholarship → ✅ apply for a scholarship
vocabulary.translation: Colocación incorrecta: gain/get → apply for a scholarship
vocabulary.example: ❌ She managed to gain a scholarship. ✅ She applied for a scholarship.

vocabulary.word: ❌ make the homologation → ✅ complete the homologation process
vocabulary.translation: make no colocaciona con procesos formales: complete/start/go through
vocabulary.example: ❌ She needs to make the homologation. ✅ complete the homologation process.

vocabulary.word: ❌ pass the documentation → ✅ submit the documentation
vocabulary.translation: submit = entregar documentos formalmente (pass = calco del español)
vocabulary.example: ❌ You need to pass the documentation. ✅ You need to submit the documentation.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: educative ≠ educativo. Correcto: education system
vocabulary.example: ❌ the educative system. ✅ the education system.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: antecedentes penales = criminal record (no penal antecedents)
vocabulary.example: ❌ He has penal antecedents. ✅ He has a criminal record.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: actual ≠ actual. actual (inglés) = real/verdadero. actual (español) = current
vocabulary.example: ❌ her actual level of English. ✅ her current level of English.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: formación = training or education (no formation)
vocabulary.example: ❌ She has a good formation. ✅ She has received excellent training.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: estudios superiores = higher education (no superior studies)
vocabulary.example: ❌ She completed her superior studies. ✅ She completed her higher education.

vocabulary.translation: False friend: compromiso = commitment. compromise (inglés) = acuerdo de concesiones mutuas
vocabulary.example: ❌ Due to my compromise with the project... ✅ Due to my commitment to the project...

vocabulary.translation: False friend: empático = empathetic. emphatic (inglés) = enfático, contundente
vocabulary.example: ❌ She is very emphatic with her students. ✅ She is very empathetic.
